arts.ttl: Defending champions kick off with win over Stuttgart
arts.sum: The German side fought back and equalised in the 68th minute with a headed goal by Undav, but the Real Madrid, Santiago Bernabéu and Champions League combination is indestructible and Ancelotti ’s side clinched victory in the 83rd minute when Rüdiger headed home Modrić’ s corner. The ball moved from one area to the other and the first to threaten were the visitors, with Leweling's low shot, after a series of rebounds, which Courtois parried away after two minutes, and a one-on-one with Millot, who failed to score in the eighth minute.
On Sunday, last season’s Bundesliga runners up Stuttgart, will be hosting the runners up of last seasons Champions League, Borussia Dortmund, with former Stuttgart players set to return to the…

arts.ttl: Weekend acca predictions: Five teams to back across Europe on Sunday

arts.ttl: Preview: Stuttgart vs. Borussia Dortmund - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Sunday's Bundesliga clash between Stuttgart and Borussia Dortmund, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Match Ratings: BVB Overcome Club Brugge to Kick Off Champions League Campaign
arts.sum: Elsewhere on the field: Marcel Sabitzer complained about playing out of position, Serhou Guirassy scored his first goal for the club, and Emre Can featured next to Pascal Groß. Ultimately it was everyone’s favorite substitute, Jamie Gittens, who notched a brace in the final fifteen minutes to secure the win for Borussia Dortmund.
